altText.1: Question 1. What does the word “muffles” mean in paragraph 3? 1 A changes to a slow speed B moves to a new stage C causes things to get darker D makes things difficult to notice
altText.2: Question 2. What is a central idea of the section “All Through the Night . . ”? A _ Falling asleep takes less than thirty minutes for most people. B __ Odd feelings while falling asleep are normal. C__ Time passes differently while falling asleep. D Falling asleep is an experience that involves the whole body.
altText.3: Question 3. Which detail best expresses a claim the author makes about sleep? 3 “Sleep is so useful that all animals do it. And without it, waking life would A be impossible.” (paragraph 2) “As you slip into sleep, your heart slows down and you breathe more B slowly.” (paragraph 4) “During deep sleep, long, slow waves of electrical impulses move through C the brain.” (paragraph 7) “In the next stage of sleep, your muscles go completely slack. Your body D doesn’t move at all.” (paragraph 8)
altText.4: Question 4. _ Based on the article, how does REM sleep help people? A __ The eyes move back and forth while they are closed. B__ The brain links experiences and memories. C People can begin having strange dreams. D__ People sleep more deeply when it is almost morning.
altText.5: Question 5. How does the author organize the information in paragraph 14? 5 A by stating a problem with sleep and then presenting a solution B by sharing a cause-and-effect situation when sleep is interrupted C by explaining the steps to learning a new game D by comparing how long people dream at night
altText.6: Question 6. Which sentence best describes how the chart adds to the information in the article? 6 A It explains how sleep helps keep the body healthy. B It shares the steps to fall asleep easily. C It offers facts about how the body reacts to sleep. D It compares being awake to being asleep.
altText.19: Question 19. How does the author organize the information in paragraphs 5 through 10? 19 A by comparing each item that Dustin pulls out of the wallet B by giving the reasons why Dustin must search for the wallet’s owner C by listing the order of events that lead to Dustin finding the wallet D by showing how the clues cause Dustin to find the wallet’s owner
altText.20: Question 20. 29 What do the details in paragraph 13 reveal about Salvador? A __ His belongings often get lost on the train. B __ He enjoys visiting the city where Dustin lives. C__sHeis grateful for Dustin’s act of kindness. D His family is very important to him.
altText.21: Question 21. Which statement best explains why Dustin claims, “This has been the best adventure ever!” in paragraph 17? A ___ Dustin's actions lead to an invitation to Rocket Land with Salvador’s family. B Marisa is proud and makes Dustin's favorite cake to celebrate. Dustin has made the day pass quickly by searching for the owner of the C wallet. D__ Salvador’s wallet has many clues to help Dustin solve the mystery.
altText.22: Question 22. What does the word “approval” suggest as used in paragraph 25? A __ Everyone is ready to leave for Rocket Land. B _ Dustin is allowed to sit with Hugo and Carlos. C The boys may eat the snacks Emerita has baked. D The blue van will take everyone to Rocket Land.
altText.23: Question 23. Which sentence best states a theme of the story? A the best mysteries are solved using teamwork. B People in different cities can have a lot in common. C Helping others can bring surprising rewards. D Pets are important for bringing families together.
altText.26: Question 26. How is paragraph 3 organized in the story? 26 A by making comparisons between two characters B by telling the solution to a problem C by giving the answer to a question D by describing events in the order they happen
altText.27: Question 27. Read this sentence from paragraph 6. 27 I rattled her courtyard fence and called, “Are you there?” What does the word “rattled” mean as used in this sentence? A made someone feel excited B noticed a door or a gate C walked around to wait for someone D made a noise by moving something
altText.28: Question 28. What do Miss Sabines’ actions in paragraph 8 reveal most about her? 28 A She cares about the narrator. B She thinks the narrator is funny. C She is a very curious person. D She likes when people visit her.
altText.29: Question 29. Which sentence from the story best describes the setting? “Even so, I enjoyed making the steep but quick (ten-minute) climb to A Miss Sabines’ house.” (paragraph 2) “It has a big garden, with beautiful desert plants and herbs growing everywhere, and a courtyard that has tiles painted bright colors, with B sculptures, pinwheels, wind chimes, and lawn ornaments scattered throughout” (paragraph 5) “The pleasant voice came floating across the courtyard walls, putting me c instantly at ease” (paragraph 7) “Miss Sabines showed me some of her new plantings, we played with her pets, and I leafed through a collection of photographs by Ansel Adams, D whom Miss Sabines calls a ‘poet of Southwestern landscapes: ” (paragraph 11)
altText.30: Question 30. Sometimes a place can be special to a person. Which detail best shows how this idea is developed in the story? “The sun was turning the Sangre de Cristo Mountains a rosy gold color.” A (paragraph 1) “Speaking of daydreams, I often fantasize about living in a house like B Miss Sabines. ” (paragraph 5) “We passed the rest of the afternoon puttering around the house.” c (paragraph 11) “We're having a party tonight, and I’ve been gone for hours!’ ” D (paragraph 12)
altText.31: Question 31. Which sentence best states a theme of the story? 31 A It is important for people to not waste their time. B Hard work always pays off in the end. C Friends can help us feel better when we are upset. D It is important to respect your elders.
